fundamentals.name: Datadog, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Datadog, Inc. operates an observability and security platform for cloud applications in the United States and internationally. The company's products comprise infrastructure and application performance monitoring, log management, observability pipelines, synthetics, real user monitoring, product analytics, continuous profiler, database monitoring, data observability, LLM observability, error tracking, network monitoring, incident response, workflow automation and App builder, event management, bits AI SRE, cloud cost management, cloud security, code security, cloud SIEM, threat management, sensitive data scanner, and CI visibility. Datadog, Inc. was incorporated in 2010 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and earnings expansion, but trades at a premium valuation and has thin operating margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates with EPS of $0.60 vs $0.51; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 32.2% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 113.4% indicate strong top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Forward P/E of 79.24, while high, is significantly lower than trailing P/E of 593.50, suggesting expected earnings growth.
Gross margin of 79.9% is very healthy, indicating efficiency in core service delivery.
Free cash flow of $936.73M TTM shows robust cash generation capabilities.
Analyst consensus is 'strong_buy' with a mean target of $223.93, suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 593.50 and Price/Sales of 21.86 indicate a very high valuation.
Operating margin of 0.8% and Profit margin of 3.7% are thin, suggesting limited profitability despite high revenue.
Return on equity of 3.9% and Return on assets of -0.2% are relatively low.
Debt/Equity ratio of 32.22, while not extremely high, indicates some leverage.

news.paragraph: Datadog is benefiting from the production phase of AI, with recent articles highlighting its potential to capture this growth. The company recently reported its first $1 billion quarter and raised its 2026 outlook, prompting a price target increase to $255 from BTIG. A golden cross technical signal also flashed, suggesting positive momentum for the stock.
